description: Block selection of milestone tasks whose acceptance criteria reference active spawned-fixup tasks (REFACTOR-N/CODE-FIX/WIRE-FIX/IMPL-FIX) — defends against forgotten --depended-on-by edges that wasted ~75 min on cbd7d081-MILESTONE-FINAL. Two-layer fix: (A) prompt-side guidance to spawn with --depended-on-by, (B-narrow) selection-side soft-dep filter that defers milestones (only) when token-aware prefix match fires.

criteria: Scoped tests for touched files pass (full suite is REVIEW-001's job)
No new unwrap()/expect() in production code paths — use map_err with TaskMgrError
No breaking changes to public APIs of selection.rs (signatures unchanged)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Loose substring matching (`task.id.contains("CODE-FIX")`) that false-positives on `CODE-FIXTURE-1` — match must require the trailing `-` boundary
Self-blocking sibling fixups (REFACTOR-N-001 deferring REFACTOR-N-002) — milestones only
Cross-PRD blocking (PRD-A's milestone blocked by PRD-B's active fixup) — `task_prefix` MUST be threaded through `get_active_task_ids`
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `select_parallel_group` excludes a milestone candidate whose AC contains `REFACTOR-N-xxx` while a `todo` `<prefix>-REFACTOR-N-001` exists
Positive: `select_parallel_group` includes the same milestone after `REFACTOR-N-001` is set to `done`
Positive: A candidate whose own ID body matches a fixup prefix (e.g. `REFACTOR-N-001`) is NEVER deferred by this filter, even when its AC mentions the prefix
Positive: Two `todo` REFACTOR-N siblings (001 + 002) with non-overlapping `touchesFiles` are BOTH returned by `select_parallel_group(.., 2)`
Positive: `get_active_task_ids` SQL includes `archived_at IS NULL` AND threads `task_prefix` via `prefix_and`
Negative: A milestone in PRD-A is NOT blocked by an active REFACTOR-N task in PRD-B (prefix scoping holds)
Negative: Archived REFACTOR-N task does NOT block a milestone
Negative: AC text `"document the CODE-FIX convention"` (no trailing dash) does NOT trigger the filter
Negative: A task whose ID is `CODE-FIXTURE-1` does NOT count as a CODE-FIX active sibling (prefix-with-dash boundary)
Negative: No new `unwrap()` / `expect()` in production paths; SQL errors propagate as `TaskMgrError`

Unit test: test_soft_dep_releases_when_refactor_n_done
Unit test: test_soft_dep_in_progress_dep_blocks
Unit test: test_soft_dep_no_match_unchanged (AC mentions but no matching active task)
Unit test: test_soft_dep_self_reference_does_not_block (REFACTOR-N-001 candidate isn't self-blocked)
Unit test: test_soft_dep_two_sibling_fixups_co_schedulable
Unit test: test_soft_dep_prefix_collision_does_not_block (CODE-FIXTURE doesn't match CODE-FIX)
Unit test: test_soft_dep_false_positive_prose_does_not_block (no trailing dash)
Unit test: test_soft_dep_other_prd_prefix_does_not_block (task_prefix scoping)
Unit test: test_soft_dep_archived_task_does_not_block
Failure mode: if `get_active_task_ids` SQL fails, the error propagates from `build_scored_candidates` (not silently swallowed)
CONTRACT: `Task.acceptance_criteria` is `Vec<String>` per `src/models/task.rs:180` — iterate, don't try to parse JSON
CONTRACT: `prefix_and(task_prefix)` returns `(clause, param)` per `src/db/prefix.rs` convention; mirror existing `get_completed_task_ids` query exactly
userStories.description:
Extend `build_scored_candidates` in `src/commands/next/selection.rs` to defer milestone-class candidates whose acceptance_criteria text mentions a known spawned-fixup prefix while a same-prefix active sibling task exists. Token-aware exact-prefix matching (no regex). Includes the new helper, the constant, the filter integration, telemetry, AND all 8 unit tests.

DO:
- Add `const SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES: &[&str] = &["REFACTOR-N", "CODE-FIX", "WIRE-FIX", "IMPL-FIX"];`
- Add `fn get_active_task_ids(conn: &Connection, task_prefix: Option<&str>) -> TaskMgrResult<HashSet<String>>` mirroring `get_completed_task_ids` (use `prefix_and` from `crate::db::prefix`, include `archived_at IS NULL`, status IN ('todo','in_progress')).
- Add `fn is_blocked_by_active_fixups(task: &Task, active_ids: &HashSet<String>) -> bool` with the three-condition logic (1: candidate-is-NOT-fixup short-circuit, 2: token-aware AC mention, 3: active-sibling-exists), skipping `task.id` itself in step 3.
- Wire a second `.filter()` after the existing dependsOn check in `build_scored_candidates`.
- On deferral, emit ONE `eprintln!("Deferring {}: AC references active fixup task(s): {}", task.id, csv)` line — matches engine.rs convention (no tracing/log imports in selection.rs).
- Tokenization: split each AC string on any character NOT in `[A-Z0-9-]` (e.g., `s.split(|c: char| !(c.is_ascii_uppercase() || c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '-'))`), check `token.starts_with(&format!("{prefix}-"))`. Same boundary check for active IDs (strip task_prefix first if needed, or compare suffix).
- Self-fixup short-circuit: if any `prefix` in `SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES` satisfies `task_id_body.starts_with(&format!("{prefix}-"))` for the candidate, return `false` immediately (sibling fixups co-schedulable).

DO NOT:
- Use loose substring matching (`task.id.contains(prefix)`) — `CODE-FIXTURE-1` would falsely match `CODE-FIX`. The trailing `-` is mandatory.
- Block sibling fixups (REFACTOR-N-001 must NOT defer REFACTOR-N-002 just because both ACs mention REFACTOR-N).
- Introduce regex / wildcard parsing — this is hard-coded prefixes only.
- Modify the public signatures of `select_next_task` or `select_parallel_group`.
- Change `selection_reason` strings (existing wording stays — soft-dep is just another reason to be ineligible).

Edge cases:
- Candidate with no acceptance_criteria → not blocked.
- Candidate AC mentions REFACTOR-N but no `REFACTOR-N-*` task exists → not blocked.
- Active sibling is `in_progress` (not `todo`) → still blocks.
- Candidate's own ID matches a fixup prefix → never blocked (self-fixup short-circuit).
- Two sibling fixups (REFACTOR-N-001 + REFACTOR-N-002) both with AC mentioning prefix → both selectable (co-schedulable).
- AC contains prose `"document the CODE-FIX convention"` (no trailing dash) → tokenizes to `CODE-FIX` (no trailing-`-` match) → not blocked.
- Active task is in another PRD's prefix → not blocked (prefix scoping in get_active_task_ids).
- Active task is archived → not blocked (archived_at IS NULL filter).

Known-bad to guard against:
- `task.id.contains("CODE-FIX")` returns true for `CODE-FIXTURE-1` — the test `test_soft_dep_prefix_collision_does_not_block` MUST FAIL this naive impl.
- A `is_blocked_by_active_fixups` that doesn't exclude `task.id` from `active_ids` would self-block any task whose own ID matches the prefix.
- An implementation that scans active_ids for full match on the token rather than prefix-with-dash would miss `REFACTOR-N-xxx → REFACTOR-N-001` correspondence.
edgeCases: AC with trailing-dash prose vs no-trailing-dash prose → only with-dash triggers
Self-reference (candidate ID body matches a fixup prefix) → short-circuit to not-blocked
Sibling fixups with same prefix → both selectable in same wave
Cross-PRD active fixups → not visible (prefix scoping)
Archived active-status tasks → not visible (archived_at filter)
Active task is `in_progress` not `todo` → still blocks

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Error propagates via TaskMgrResult — not silently swallowed; build_scored_candidates returns Err

failureModes.cause: Candidate has malformed acceptance_criteria (parse failure upstream)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Task struct already defaults to Vec::new() per src/models/task.rs:320 — filter sees empty AC and is a no-op
Skip task.id when scanning — prevents self-block

userStories.notes: Reuse: `get_completed_task_ids` (existing in selection.rs:188) as the template for `get_active_task_ids` — same prefix_and helper, same query_map shape. Reuse: `Task` struct from `crate::models::Task`, already imported. Test helpers: `setup_test_db()` + `insert_task_full(conn, id, title, status, priority, description, criteria)` from `src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs:138` accept criteria as `&[&str]` — perfect for AC text. For archived tests: insert_task_full doesn't set archived_at, so use raw SQL in the archived test (`UPDATE tasks SET archived_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`). For task_prefix tests: pass `task_prefix=Some("PRD-A-")` to select_parallel_group; insert tasks with both prefixes. Learning [1855]: select_parallel_group already documented as the parallel selection entry point. Learning [1789]: prior selection.rs work used borrowed-not-moved file references — preserve. Learning [538]: prefix-scoped queries need archived_at IS NULL too (mirror `get_completed_task_ids`). Performance: O(candidates × AC_lines × tokens × 4 prefixes) — sub-ms for documented 100-200 task PRDs.
qualityDimensions: Token-aware exact-prefix matching with mandatory trailing dash — never loose substring
Self-fixup short-circuit prevents sibling co-blocking
task_prefix threaded so cross-PRD bleed is impossible
archived_at IS NULL mirrored from get_completed_task_ids — archived tasks never block
Single eprintln per deferral (not per candidate) — operator visibility without spam
No public signature changes to select_next_task / select_parallel_group
No new dependencies (no regex crate, no log/tracing) — fits existing selection.rs imports

userStories.description:
Add an end-to-end integration test in `tests/task_selection.rs` that reproduces the production bug from `cbd7d081-MILESTONE-FINAL` to lock in the fix.

DO:
- Insert MILESTONE-FINAL with the real-world acceptance_criteria text including 'REFACTOR-N-xxx if any' and 'Any spawned CODE-FIX/WIRE-FIX/IMPL-FIX/REFACTOR-N tasks have passes=true'.
- Insert active siblings: `<prefix>-REFACTOR-N-001` (in_progress) and `<prefix>-REFACTOR-N-002` (todo), both with distinct `touchesFiles` so they're co-schedulable in a parallel group.
- Call `select_parallel_group(&conn, &[], Some("<prefix>-"), 2)`.
- Assert MILESTONE-FINAL is NOT in the returned group.
- Assert REFACTOR-N-002 IS in the returned group (REFACTOR-N-001 is in_progress, so it's not a `todo` candidate at all — that's expected).
- After marking both REFACTOR-N tasks `done`, re-run select and assert MILESTONE-FINAL IS now returned.

DO NOT:
- Re-test unit-level behavior already covered by FEAT-001's unit tests (this is the integration check, not a substitute).
- Mock `select_parallel_group` — exercise the real function against a real SQLite connection.
edgeCases: REFACTOR-N-001 is in_progress → not even a todo candidate, MILESTONE deferred via REFACTOR-N-002
Both REFACTOR-N tasks done → MILESTONE eligible

failureModes.cause: Test asserts MILESTONE-FINAL appears even though it shouldn't
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test fails — caught by CI before regression ships

Touches `src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs` ONLY if a new helper is needed (e.g. `insert_task_with_archive_at`); otherwise reuse `insert_task_full`.

userStories.description:
Update `src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/task_ops.rs` so the loop agent learns to pass `--depended-on-by <milestone-id>` whenever it spawns a fix in response to a milestone task's acceptance criteria — the durable root-cause fix.

DO:
- Insert a new bullet immediately after the existing `task-mgr add --stdin` example, with this content (rendered) ≈300 bytes:

  > - **Spawning a fix in response to a milestone's AC**: if you're creating
  >   this fix because some milestone task's acceptance criteria called for
  >   spawned `CODE-FIX-…`/`WIRE-FIX-…`/`IMPL-FIX-…`/`REFACTOR-N-…` tasks,
  >   pass `--depended-on-by <that-milestone-id>` so the loop won't dispatch
  >   the milestone before your fix lands:
  >
  >       echo '{"id":"REFACTOR-N-001",...}' | task-mgr add --stdin \
  >         --depended-on-by MILESTONE-FINAL

- Update the unit test `test_section_contains_critical_phrases` to assert the new content is present.
- Verify `test_section_size_within_budget` (asserts < 1536 bytes) still passes — measure post-change with a `dbg!()` or `println!()` in a one-shot test run, then remove.

DO NOT:
- Reflow or reword the existing bullets — surgical addition only.
- Bloat the section beyond ~300 bytes net add (current section is well under budget; ~300 bytes leaves ample headroom).
- Add a new test file — extend the existing tests in `task_ops.rs`.
- Add references to the soft-dep filter (FEAT-001) — the prompt should stand on its own as the root-cause guidance; the filter is defense-in-depth, not the contract.
edgeCases: Section size approaches 1536 bytes → fail fast in the existing test

Existing tests are at lines 41-105; extend `test_section_contains_critical_phrases` (line 44). The 1536-byte budget is asserted by `test_section_size_within_budget` (line 98).

acceptanceCriteria: Each helper that replaces find_blocking_active_fixups is under 30 body lines
is_blocked_by_active_fixups removed; no other callers exist (grep -rn confirms)

format! needle strings computed once per tokenization call, not per token

cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --lib selection all clean
userStories.description:
From REFACTOR-001: src/commands/next/selection.rs:57-103 + 168-185. Three coupled smells fixed in one refactor.

1) find_blocking_active_fixups body is ~37 lines doing three distinct phases: self-fixup short-circuit, AC tokenization, active-sibling match. Borderline against the explicit 'under 30 lines' AC. Split into smaller helpers, e.g. task_id_is_self_fixup(task) -> bool, mentioned_fixup_prefixes(task) -> Vec<&'static str>, and find_active_blockers_for_prefixes(prefixes, active_ids, task_id) -> Vec<String>. The orchestrator becomes ~10 lines.

2) is_blocked_by_active_fixups (selection.rs:101-103) is a thin wrapper used only by the filter at selection.rs:172. The filter calls the wrapper to check if blocked, then calls find_blocking_active_fixups AGAIN at line 178 to get blockers for the eprintln — wasted double-call on blocked items. Drop the wrapper and use the result of one call: let blockers = find_blocking_active_fixups(task, &active_ids); if blockers.is_empty() { return true; } eprintln!(...); false.

3) Inside the tokenizer (selection.rs:69) format!("{prefix}-") is rebuilt for every (AC × token × prefix). Pre-compute the needles once at the start of mentioned_fixup_prefixes (e.g. let needles: Vec<String> = SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES.iter().map(|p| format!("{p}-")).collect()). Naturally falls out of the split.
userStories.notes: Bundled because all three smells live in the same ~50-line region and one coherent split addresses all of them.
userStories.title: Split find_blocking_active_fixups; drop wrapper; pre-compute needles
